name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Defend our children (DeVos)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2654/7377f838/362c733c/51fb45a3/2691825917/VEsH/ "http://www.joeforindiana.com/")

| | Peter,

Earlier this week, President Trump and Secretary of Education DeVos unveiled their education budget and it could have devastating effects on America's public schools -- including those right here in Indiana.

Under DeVos and Trump's proposal, **our education system would suffer $9.2 billion in budget cuts.** This proposal is dangerous, irresponsible, and eliminates the opportunity for our students to achieve their true potential. In addition to being irresponsible, it's indefensible. When asked if she would not allow federal funds to go toward private schools who refuse to admit LGBT students, Secretary DeVos _refused_ to answer.

**Our kids need better -- they deserve better -- and we must stand up and tell the Trump administration that our children are off limits.**

**[Will you add your name to stand with me, defend our children, and tell the Trump administration: Hands off our kids' futures?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2654/7377f838/362c733c/51fb45a2/2691825917/VEsE/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/stand-with-joe-to-tell-betsy-devos-and-the-trump-administration-education-is-non-negotiable-")**

Peter, I told you when Secretary DeVos was confirmed that I would do everything I could to ask tough questions and speak out loudly if she put partisan ideology ahead of what's best for our kids. **This is that moment.** If the proposed budget is implemented, it will cut:
* Student loan forgiveness programs for public servants
* Teacher training
* After-school programs serving low-income students
* Vocational and technical education programs
* Mental health services
* Class size reduction funding

It's no secret that cutting these programs would have devastating effects on our children's future. Teachers shouldn't have to work multiple jobs to make ends meet, working families deserve to know that their child has a safe place to go after school, and young people should be able to pursue a degree without the fear that student debt will prevent them from owning a home or pursuing advanced degrees.

**As your senator, I promise to continue to fight for our all of our kids. I hope you 'll stand with me by adding your name today.**

**[Tell the Trump administration: We can't afford to gamble with our children's futures.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2654/7377f838/362c733c/51fb45a2/2691825917/VEsF/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/stand-with-joe-to-tell-betsy-devos-and-the-trump-administration-education-is-non-negotiable-")**

Thank you for standing with me,

Joe **** **** **** **** | [**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2654/7377f838/362c733c/51fb459d/2691825917/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-donate?refcode=20170526db&amount=25")
---
**** ****
**** ****

|
---

Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
---

**_Racing Toward the Checkered Flag_**
Governor **Eric Holcomb** is pretty fond of racing analogies, so in recognition of this being race week, I thought I would use one, too. This week saw story after story after story proving that we're not just in the pole position, as Governor Holcomb likes to say, but we're racing hard toward the checkered flag. Multiple companies announced plans to locate operations in Indiana while some established firms said they are on the path to more growth. We're seeing a jobs explosion in Indiana and it's all thanks to proven Republican leadership. Other states want to pass us, but we have no plans to take our foot off the gas.

\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer with Indiana Young Republican Chairwoman **Abby Weingardt** and Indiana College Republican Chairman **Roger Howard** following the Indiana Young Republican State Convention on Saturday, May 20, at State Party Headquarters.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
| Jobs, jobs, jobs. It was a great week of news for Indiana's economy as Salesforce [officially opened their regional headquarters](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=00faed09e9&e=1acd9bb3d6) in downtown Indianapolis; company CEO Bob Stutz called Indianapolis "[the best kept secret in the country](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d1e96175f8&e=1acd9bb3d6)"; Genesys, which recently acquired Interactive Intelligence, announced [plans to grow again in Indiana soon](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f73c71d315&e=1acd9bb3d6); and Infosys [unveiled plans](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=32b74c6d2a&e=1acd9bb3d6) to locate their first U.S.-based tech hub in Indiana, a project that will add 2,000 jobs.

Governor Holcomb also took the [jobs and growth message to Fort Wayne](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=34dd2cb70e&e=1acd9bb3d6) where he attended the Northern Indiana Conservation Project Banquet on Thursday. "Folks are looking for cool places to live, with a lot of different amenities, and that’s where we can really excel, and that’s where our state parks and all of our facilities quite frankly really matter. That’s what people are thinking about, is quality of place and quality of life and having access to the outdoors like never before."

In non-jobs news, be sure you don't miss these two great articles about Governor Holcomb's laser-focus on our state's drug epidemic.

**Indianapolis Star** : [Holcomb won't look away from opioid crisis](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8211909be2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**South Bend Tribune** : [Drug plan is first step in long journey](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1f580a30ec&e=1acd9bb3d6)

(Photo: Governor Holcomb checks out the Rolling Thunder "motorcade" before they head to Washington, D.C., for Memorial Day Weekend.)

---
|
---
| |
---
_**A Note from Barb McClellan**_
_As we reported in last week's Party Line, we honored Barb McClellan for her 50+ years of service to the Indiana Republican Party at an event attended by six former State Party chairs, including Gov. Holcomb, two former vice chairs and dozens of supporters. Below is a note from Barb to all those who attended and sent notes to her over the past few weeks._

**"My sincere appreciation goes out to all who attended my open house on May 15th. Your kind words, best wishes, and acts of kindness meant a lot to me. To all former officers, staff members, elected officials, business associates, family and friends...you are special. Thank you for the fond memories."**
\- Barb McClellan
---
|
---
|

_**New Report Brings More Bad News for Joe Donnelly's Beloved Obamacare**_
A [new report from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c2041eacef&e=1acd9bb3d6) shows that the health care law Joe Donnelly has supported, defended and championed since the day he voted for it in 2010 brought Hoosiers a 74 percent increase, or an average of $178 per month, in monthly health care premiums from 2013 to 2017.

Joe Donnelly must be held to account for this massive increase in health insurance premiums on Hoosiers. His declarations of wanting to fix Obamacare ring hallow when he continues to support, defend and champion a law that is so obviously wreaking havoc on our economy and our health care system.

Click here to read [more](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bf7e95e94a&e=1acd9bb3d6).

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: This isn't impossible: We could flip the House
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7956/24c69fd9/e672d507/18e4ead5/4038272062/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact")
---

Peter,

How does taking back the House sound?

I'm here to tell you: It's doable.

Americans are sick and tired of Republican policies that hurt women and families. We're angry at representatives who won't stand up to Donald Trump and his harmful policies, like his budget that would strip Planned Parenthood of any and all federal funding, his health care bill that would kick millions off their insurance, or his plans to gut Medicaid.

I know it seems early, and it won't be easy with districts heavily gerrymandered in favor of Republicans. But when this community works together, we can do incredible things.

**[We can only flip the House next year if everyone pitches in. Donate $3 to unseat Republicans, elect pro-choice Democratic women, and flip the House from red to blue.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7956/24c69fd9/e672d507/18e4ead6/4038272062/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170523_email_FlipTheHouse?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+We+can+only+flip+the+House+next+year+if+&utm_campaign=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact")**

We need to flip 24 seats to take back the House. That's a number within our reach.

For starters, Trump's approval rating has fallen to a new low of just 41%. The first midterm election of a presidency is, historically, not a great election for the party that holds the White House, and Trump's low approval ratings could sink other Republicans.

On top of that, 217 House Republicans voted for Trumpcare, a bill that would take health care away from millions while giving tax breaks to the rich.

But it's not just about Republicans' shortcomings: All across the country, grassroots Democrats are getting fired up, marching in the streets, connecting with their communities, and considering running for office. At EMILY's List, more than 13,000 women have told us they're interested in running. And nearly 7,000 people have told us they want to help a woman run.

We're using these unprecedented numbers to encourage more women to jump into House races. And if we can grow our recruitment roster and sustain this grassroots energy, we could build a wave big enough to flip those 24 seats and turn the House blue.

**Now is a crucial time to build our power. Donate $3 to make sure we have the resources to elect pro-choice Democratic women and take back the House next year:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Flip-The-House](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7956/24c69fd9/e672d507/18e4ead7/4038272062/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170523_email_FlipTheHouse?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgFlipTheHouse&utm_campaign=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact")**

Thank you,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7956/24c69fd9/e672d507/18e4ead0/4038272062/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170523_email_FlipTheHouse?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFE530N_digital_email_20170526_FlipTheHouse_inact")

subject: Your Weekly NH GOP Newsletter
body:

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, May 26th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
This past week we had two special elections, one in Wolfeboro with Matthew Plache and the other in Hillsborough 44 with Mark McLean. Both candidates worked hard to win these House seats and I sincerely appreciate their willingness to step up to serve their communities. While we did not prevail in Wolfeboro, I am confident that in 2018 we will take that seat back.
In the meantime, we will have multiple special elections for the State House and one for the State Senate to defend or take back.
We won and maintained our majority in 2016 because we had great candidates and a winning message. We can continue to win in the special elections with your help. We know grassroots effort works – knocking on doors, talking with our neighbors, getting people out to vote.
In the coming weeks, we will be working hard here at the NHGOP to get these Republicans elected to office and our call to action to you is to help us make those wins a reality!
As we head into the Memorial Day weekend, please take some time to remember those brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice so that we can be a free nation.
Wishing you a peaceful Memorial Day Weekend,
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

| **Board Decision Dismisses Union Complaints, Affirms Governor Sununu’s Position**
Concord, NH – Today the Public Employee Labor Relations Board (PELRB) issued a decision dismissing unfair labor practice complaints filed against the State by the State Employees Association (SEA) and the New England Police Benevolent Association (NEPBA). The decision affirms the Governor’s position that the State’s five public employee unions are required to use one union bargaining committee to negotiate common terms and conditions of employment.
The proceedings before the PELRB arose when the SEA and NEPBA filed unfair labor practice complaints in March charging that the state improperly left the bargaining table after the Teamsters and New Hampshire Troopers Association (NHTA) declared impasse. The PELRB rejected these charges, and found that the State acted appropriately and in compliance with state labor laws. The PELRB ordered the five unions to coordinate and come to agreement on whether to return to the bargaining table or proceed to mediation or fact finding.
In response to the PELRB’s decision, Matt Newland, Manager of Employee Relations and Chairman of the State of New Hampshire Negotiating Team stated the following: “Today’s decision affirms that our actions have been appropriate and in compliance with the statute. We continue to stand ready once the unions decide how they would like to proceed. I am confident that we can reach an agreement that is fair to both our hard working state employees and New Hampshire’s taxpayers.”

**Bradley praises Daniels leadership, responsible Senate budget**
**Concord, NH –** Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statement:
“Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels has been a true leader throughout the Senate’s budget process, ensuring all voices were heard in the public hearing and working with advocates, members of the community and colleagues to produce a responsible budget for New Hampshire.”
“The Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget lives within our means while providing funding for the needs in our communities, including mental health, substance abuse, families with a disabled child, and abused and neglected children. It also serves to grow jobs and drive our state’s economy through tax relief for small businesses, ensuring that New Hampshire is a competitive state to grow and expand business.”

* * *
**Senate Finance approves business tax cuts, capital investment** **deductions supporting NH job creators**
**Concord, NH -** The Senate Finance Committee today approved reductions to the Business Enterprise Tax [BET] and Business Profits Tax [BPT] as part of the Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget. The BET would be lowered to .60 percent in 2019 and then .50 percent in 2021 while the BPT in 2019 would be at 7.7 percent and in 2021 decrease to 7.5 percent.
Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford), Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield) and Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren) issued the following statements:
“I am pleased that the Senate Finance Committee adopted additional tax cuts for small businesses and job creators in our state,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “Two years ago, after cutting business taxes for the first time in over 20 years, we saw a dramatic uptick in job creation and businesses continue to grow and expand, improving our state’s economy to this day.”
“The business cuts in the last budget have proven beneficial for our state’s small business community, yet, in order to remain competitive with other states in New England and across the country we need to continue lowering business taxes and creating a strong environment for job creation,” said Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren).
The committee also authorized raising the tax deduction for capital purchases, Section 179 Deduction, bringing New Hampshire in line with the Federal guidelines for deductable capital purchases up to $500,000.
“Allowing growing businesses to make deductions on capital investments up to $500,000 will provide them with a greater incentive to grow and expand in New Hampshire, creating even more good jobs for graduates and those raising families in our state,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield). “Furthering cuts to business taxes and incentivizing businesses to grow and expand provide an increasingly competitive business environment in New Hampshire that says to businesses considering relocation that the Granite State is open for business.”
“As part of the budget process, we’ve balanced the needs of our citizens while ensuring that we will continue to live within our means and I commend the Senate Finance committee for their dedication to making our state a great place to live and to do business,” added Daniels.

Chris Sununu, and Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper all were in attendance and supported the CADY Regional Prevention Summit today | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on Jay Lucas! Jay was nominated by Augusta Petrone for his outstanding and selfless work. Jay, who once ran for Governor in NH, is heading up an effort to provide scholarships for children of fallen Military members. His organization is making New Hampshire its launching point as they work to grow nation-wide. We thank him for his continued dedication to the Party and tireless work toward helping the families of those who have given their lives in the name of freedom. Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Governor's Corner | ---|---|--- | Board Decision Dismisses Union Complaints, Affirms Governor Sununu’s Position Concord, NH – Today the Public Employee Labor Relations Board (PELRB) issued a decision dismissing unfair labor practice complaints filed against the State by the State Employees Association (SEA) and the New England Police Benevolent Association (NEPBA). The decision affirms the Governor’s position that the State’s five public employee unions are required to use one union bargaining committee to negotiate common terms and conditions of employment. The proceedings before the PELRB arose when the SEA and NEPBA filed unfair labor practice complaints in March charging that the state improperly left the bargaining table after the Teamsters and New Hampshire Troopers Association (NHTA) declared impasse. The PELRB rejected these charges, and found that the State acted appropriately and in compliance with state labor laws. The PELRB ordered the five unions to coordinate and come to agreement on whether to return to the bargaining table or proceed to mediation or fact finding. In response to the PELRB’s decision, Matt Newland, Manager of Employee Relations and Chairman of the State of New Hampshire Negotiating Team stated the following: “Today’s decision affirms that our actions have been appropriate and in compliance with the statute. We continue to stand ready once the unions decide how they would like to proceed. I am confident that we can reach an agreement that is fair to both our hard working state employees and New Hampshire’s taxpayers.” | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans Senate Bill 3 will be up for a vote in the House of Representatives Thursday. Rep. Greg Hill (R-Northfield) explains in the House Calendar that, “This bill as amended by the committee is designed to strengthen public confidence in the integrity of our elections by closing the domicile loophole. The Republican majority believes that if current law creates opportunities for voter fraud we believe that the law should be changed to eliminate those opportunities...” Republicans on the House Election Law committee published an op-ed . Earlier in the week, election law experts from the Heritage Foundation published an op-ed in the Concord Monitor in support of SB3,. and ask them to support Senate Bill 3 on June 1st. | | Senate Republicans Bradley praises Daniels leadership, responsible Senate budget Concord, NH – Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statement: “Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels has been a true leader throughout the Senate’s budget process, ensuring all voices were heard in the public hearing and working with advocates, members of the community and colleagues to produce a responsible budget for New Hampshire.” “The Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget lives within our means while providing funding for the needs in our communities, including mental health, substance abuse, families with a disabled child, and abused and neglected children. It also serves to grow jobs and drive our state’s economy through tax relief for small businesses, ensuring that New Hampshire is a competitive state to grow and expand business.” Senate Finance approves business tax cuts, capital investment deductions supporting NH job creators Concord, NH - The Senate Finance Committee today approved reductions to the Business Enterprise Tax [BET] and Business Profits Tax [BPT] as part of the Fiscal Year 2018-19 budget. The BET would be lowered to .60 percent in 2019 and then .50 percent in 2021 while the BPT in 2019 would be at 7.7 percent and in 2021 decrease to 7.5 percent. Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford), Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield) and Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren) issued the following statements: “I am pleased that the Senate Finance Committee adopted additional tax cuts for small businesses and job creators in our state,” said Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford). “Two years ago, after cutting business taxes for the first time in over 20 years, we saw a dramatic uptick in job creation and businesses continue to grow and expand, improving our state’s economy to this day.” “The business cuts in the last budget have proven beneficial for our state’s small business community, yet, in order to remain competitive with other states in New England and across the country we need to continue lowering business taxes and creating a strong environment for job creation,” said Senator Bob Giuda (R-Warren). The committee also authorized raising the tax deduction for capital purchases, Section 179 Deduction, bringing New Hampshire in line with the Federal guidelines for deductable capital purchases up to $500,000. “Allowing growing businesses to make deductions on capital investments up to $500,000 will provide them with a greater incentive to grow and expand in New Hampshire, creating even more good jobs for graduates and those raising families in our state,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield). “Furthering cuts to business taxes and incentivizing businesses to grow and expand provide an increasingly competitive business environment in New Hampshire that says to businesses considering relocation that the Granite State is open for business.” “As part of the budget process, we’ve balanced the needs of our citizens while ensuring that we will continue to live within our means and I commend the Senate Finance committee for their dedication to making our state a great place to live and to do business,” added Daniels. | | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director -** | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? In Case You Missed It:

"Teachers, Democrats Throw Taxpayers Under the Bus"

Caledonian Record
May 20, 2017

On Thursday, Vermont taxpayers saw exactly what Vermont NEA candidate campaign contributions to Democrats and a fully staffed, $2 million bullying operation in Montpelier is good for.

After nearly five weeks (two of which weren’t expected, and at an extra cost to taxpayers) of drama concerning the implementation of a statewide teacher healthcare contract, Speaker Mitzi Johnson and President Pro Tem Tim Ashe presented their grand plan to achieve the $26 million in savings that Governor Scott has demanded.

The punchline? A study and a full-throated, $6 million pillage from Education Fund reserves to create the illusion of a property tax cut in FY18.

It sounds like a joke, but every Democrat in the House and Senate voted for it... including local Senators Kitchel and Starr (Rodgers was absent), and Representatives Toll and Troiano. Thankfully, local Republican legislators all voted against this folly.

Fortunately, Governor Phil Scott intends to use his veto, and he made that point perfectly clear as the Legislature adjourned after midnight.

How this standoff will end up is anyone’s guess. We hope that, ultimately, public opinion carries the day. But we’ve been around long enough to realize that the Democratic majority cares little about the public when it comes to spending our money wisely. We’re fully cognizant that Democrats bow to the NEA in exchange for huge campaign contributions.

The rhetoric and misinformation from Vermont NEA and local Education Associations across Vermont will go into hyper drive until the Veto Session on June 21st. They will bring out their tired and flatly untrue arguments: teachers are underpaid, collective bargaining at the district level is sacred, and unions are under attack.

For us, this issue is simple. By moving to statewide collective bargaining (on a short-term, for healthcare only), Vermont can provide its school employees the same excellent care they currently receive at no additional cost to them, and save districts and taxpayers $26 million on an annual basis.

Given the enormous pressure property taxpayers are under, we are hopeful that Johnson and Ashe will embrace their leadership role and quietly let Vermont NEA know that the Vermont Democratic Party has finally decided to move Vermont taxpayers to the head of the line.

Read the original editorial in the Caledonian Record by clicking here (http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=685bd08ad7&e=19cebfe173) .

subject: We're ready to keep fighting
body:
We need to be ready to fight for every single vote, no matter where that fight takes us, and Our Revolution will be there to help progressives win in every way that we can.
#
Friends,
The past two weeks have been a rollercoaster. We saw our endorsed candidates win in unlikely places--Larry Krasner was elected to Philadelphia District Attorney defeating a corrupt party patronage system and Christine Pellegrino won a State Assembly seat in a district that voted for Trump. While yesterday's race in Montana did not turn out the way we had hoped, our efforts made a huge difference. There is real support for our progressive agenda as we fight to change the systems of power.
There will be many more races in the months to come, in red states, blue states, and those that are right on the tipping point. We need to be ready to fight for every single vote, no matter where that fight takes us, and Our Revolution will be there to help progressives win in every way that we can.
**This wouldn't be possible without your support.[We're going to continue building this grassroots movement. Can you spare $5 to help Our Revolution organize across the country?](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d5a/43f17890/d66cd8e0/620380e6/706884993/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/everyvote?refcode=em170526-full")**
The most important thing that we can do right now is decide how best we can continue to build the political revolution. Real progressive values are strongly competing in important races all over our country, and we must continue to support them. The surface of what our movement is capable of has not yet even been scratched.
Sustained growth and increased voter turnout isn't going to come from campaigns that parachute in three months before election day and then leave once the ballots are cast. Winning trust is hard work. It takes talking to voters about issues they care about, and solutions that they believe in. We've only just begun launching state and local groups across the country, and already Our Revolution members are creating change from the bottom up, changing the fabric of the Democratic Party and inspiring new participants to get involved in the political process. It takes time to build, but make no mistake: we're making an impact right now.
**[We're dedicated to keep fighting for the future we all believe in. Please chip in $5 to show that you're ready to keep fighting too.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d5a/43f17890/d66cd8e0/620380e6/706884993/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/everyvote?refcode=em170526-full")**
Your efforts drive this movement. Thank you for everything you do.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution
